figures.caption: Fig. 1. An example uninformative trajectory for aided inertial navigation with delayed aiding measurements. The vehicle follows a helical-type motion, shown in red, generated by a constant element of the Lie algebra sgal(3). Body-frame axes are plotted along the trajectory. The grey curves are projections of the trajectory onto the coordinate planes and show its overall shape. For motions in this class, the delayed measurement history does not uniquely determine the unknown delay and initial navigation state.

Fig. 2. Examples of several subfamilies of uninformative trajectories generated by constant body-frame inputs. Clockwise from top left: straight-line accelerated motion, with constant inertial-frame acceleration and with the body rotating about the acceleration direction; a coordinated turn in the xy-plane at constant bank angle; a climbing coordinated turn, with circular horizontal motion and constant vertical velocity in the inertial frame; and a skew helical trajectory, produced by the coupling of constant body-frame angular velocity and constant body-frame acceleration. Body-frame axes are plotted along each trajectory, with red x forward, green y right, and blue z up. Each curve is shaded according to the magnitude of the inertial-frame velocity, while the grey projections onto the coordinate planes show the trajectory shape.
